text: 🇮🇷 Iran’s army: In recent hours, Iran targeted Al-Kharj Base and Al-Adairy Camp using Arash-2 strike drones. Due to the deployment of ground units, special forces, and the “Night Stalkers” helicopter unit, Al-Adairy Camp—located north of Kuwait—has played a key role in recent enemy operations in southern Isfahan. The “Night Stalkers” unit was formed after the U.S. defeat in Operation Eagle Claw (Tabas).

The military says close coordination between ground troops and the Air Force has enabled rapid targeting of threats, with strikes conducted in real time to neutralise militant activity. @wfwitness

🇮🇱 💥 🇮🇷 Israeli Air Force carried out strikes on a petrochemical facility in Assaluyeh, part of Iran’s South Pars gas field infrastructure. Earlier, the same South Pars complex in Assaluyeh was hit on March 18, targeting a gas processing plant. Israeli Occupation Minister Israel Katz stated that both facilities, which he claims account for around 85% of Iran’s oil and gas chemical exports, have been disabled and are no longer operational. He added that the IOF has been ordered to continue strikes on Iran’s national infrastructure. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

INTERESTING: A field report claims oil markets may be misreading disruption in the Strait of Hormuz, because a large share of tanker traffic is hidden from tracking systems. A Citrini Research analyst went on-the-ground with: — $15K cash — recording sunglasses — Cuban cigars (for networking) — smuggled camera + audio gear — Crossed into Oman, signed a pledge not to gather intel — Observed traffic from a boat ~18 miles off Iran, with drones overhead. Key findings — ~50% of ships missing from AIS tracking. — Tankers go “dark” or spoof locations/ownership. — Iran’s “ghost fleet” still moving oil. — ~$3 billion+ crude transported during the conflict, much toward Asia.

⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇮🇷 Residents of Tabriz, in Iran's West Azebaijan province, recorded thick black smoke over the city after an airstrike, likely Israeli, targeted an industrial area to the west of the city early this Monday morning. @wfwitness

🇮🇷 Iran has delivered its response to a U.S. proposal for ending hostilities, according to IRNA, outlining a 10-point framework. Tehran’s conditions reportedly include a full cessation of military operations, guarantees for safe maritime passage through the Strait of Hormuz, and the lifting of sanctions. Iran also rejected the idea of a temporary ceasefire, stating that only a permanent end to the conflict is acceptable, IRNA reports. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

⚡️ 🇮🇷 🇵🇰 🇺🇸 IRNA: Iran has submitted a proposed framework to end the war to the U.S. via Pakistan. The proposal reportedly consists of ten clauses and rejects a short-term truce, instead calling for a lasting end to the conflict based on Iran’s terms and security considerations. Among the key demands are an end to regional hostilities, guarantees for safe navigation through the Strait of Hormuz, reconstruction measures, and the lifting of sanctions. @wfwitness

🇷🇺 ☦️ Junior Lieutenant Nikolai Sergeevich Sharapov (“Veles”), 1994–2026. A war correspondent, special forces reconnaissance officer, and UAV operator. He took part in the conflict from 2022, serving in reconnaissance units of the 2nd Army, “Center” group, operating on some of the most intense sectors of the front. He commanded a reconnaissance group, worked with drones, and was known among his comrades as a disciplined, capable, and steady officer. He also wrote the book “Through My Own Eyes” about the war in Ukraine, which later received the Arsenyev Literary Prize (2024). Fell in action while performing his duty. Known to friends and colleagues as “Veles” — a professional soldier, writer, and man who stayed in the fight until the end. Eternal glory to the hero! 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

Mehrabad Airport, targeted multiple times during Operation Roaring Lion, was used by the Quds Force as a hub for arming and financing the "regime’s proxies" across the Middle East. Israel will supply Greece with rocket launchers and munitions in a €650 million deal. The agreement, signed in Athens, includes Elbit Systems Ltd’s PULS rocket artillery system. The package includes launchers, training rockets, precision-guided missiles, and loitering munitions.

Open Source Intel Video shows a U.S. Air Force C-295W from the 427th Special Operations Squadron flying low over the western Iranian desert. The aircraft was part of the exfiltration effort for special forces operating behind enemy lines during the rescue mission. The C-295W is a rare special operations platform used by the 427th SOS under AFSOC. Only a small number are in service. Built by Airbus in Spain, it is designed for short and unprepared runways, low level flight, and insertion and extraction missions in austere environments. tweet

Senator Graham: It must be clear that when this war is over - either via diplomacy or military action - that control of the Strait of Hormuz does not lie with Iran. This war must end in a fashion — either through diplomacy or military action — that prevents Iran from going back to their old ways.

Chinese AI firms are using satellite data + open-source intel to map U.S. military assets, and that data may be helping Iran improve targeting. Companies like MizarVision publish AI-enhanced maps of U.S. bases, aircraft, and ships. Data comes from commercial satellites + public tracking tools. U.S. intel believes Iran (IRGC) may use it to plan missile/drone strikes more precisely. Source: ABC Exclusive
